notes: The root ر س ل carries the idea of sending — specifically, being sent on a mission with a message. In the Quran, it appears overwhelmingly in the context of prophets as God's messengers (رَسُول) and the act of God sending them (أَرْسَلَ). You already know this word from the second shahādah: أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا رَسُولُ ٱللَّهِ.

forms.notes: The primary form — appears 332 times. The word every Muslim knows from the shahādah.
The verb behind the noun. Allah sent messengers — appears in many prophet narratives.
Often plural. Surah Al-Mursalat (77) is named after this word.
The message itself — what the messenger carries.
Rare — refers to Allah as the sender.
